steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
I need help setting up a git repository for tracking configuration files on my system. Here's what I need you to do:

1. Initialize a new git repository in the directory `/home/user/config-tracking`

2. Create a configuration file called `app.conf` inside that directory with the following exact content:
```
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
```

3. Add this configuration file to git staging and make an initial commit with the exact commit message: `Initial configuration setup`

4. After completing these steps, create a log file at `/home/user/config-tracking/setup.log` that contains the following information in this exact format:
   - Line 1: The full path to the git repository
   - Line 2: The name of the tracked configuration file
   - Line 3: The commit hash (full 40-character SHA) of the initial commit
   - Line 4: The exact commit message used

Each piece of information should be on its own line with no extra whitespace or formatting. The log file should have exactly 4 lines.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user#

Analysis: The terminal is currently in the `/home/user` directory as the root user. No work has been done yet. I need to create the directory structure, initialize git, create the configuration file, commit it, and then create the log file with repository information.
Plan: First, I'll create the config-tracking directory and initialize git. Then create the app.conf file with the specified content. After that, configure git user info (required for commits), stage and commit the file. Finally, create the setup.log file with the repository information including the commit hash.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > app.conf << 'EOF'
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
EOF

results.content:
Previous response had warnings:
WARNINGS: - Extra text detected before JSON object
- Extra text detected after JSON object

root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user# cd /home/user/config-tracking
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git init

hint: is subject to change. To configure the initial branch name to use in all
hint: of your new repositories, which will suppress this warning, call:
hint:
hint:   git config --global init.defaultBranch <name>
hint:

hint: 'development'. The just-created branch can be renamed via this command:
hint:
hint:   git branch -m <name>
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/user/config-tracking/.git/

> [database]
> host=localhost
> port=5432
> name=production_db
>
> [logging]
> level=INFO
> path=/var/log/app.log
> EOF

[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git config user.email "user@example.com"
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git config user.name "User"
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git add app.conf
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git commit -m "Initial configuration setup"
[master (root-commit) 1e1e103] Initial configuration setup
 1 file changed, 8 insertions(+)

root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git log --format=%H -n 1
1e1e1038504dfcd55a7df4595e640ae8ab64ca6e
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking#

steps.message:
Analysis: Great progress! The git repository has been initialized, the app.conf file has been created with the correct content, and the initial commit has been made with the exact commit message 'Initial configuration setup'. The commit hash is 1e1e1038504dfcd55a7df4595e640ae8ab64ca6e. Now I need to create the setup.log file with the required 4 lines of information.
Plan: Create the setup.log file with exactly 4 lines: 1) Full path to the git repository (/home/user/config-tracking), 2) Name of the tracked configuration file (app.conf), 3) The full 40-character commit hash (1e1e1038504dfcd55a7df4595e640ae8ab64ca6e), 4) The exact commit message (Initial configuration setup). I'll use echo commands to create this file, then verify its contents.

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# echo "/home/user/config-tracking" > setup.log
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# echo "app.conf" >> setup.log
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git log --format=%H -n 1 >> setup.log
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# git log --format=%s -n 1 >> setup.log
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# cat setup.log
/home/user/config-tracking
app.conf
1e1e1038504dfcd55a7df4595e640ae8ab64ca6e
Initial configuration setup
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking# wc -l setup.log
4 setup.log
root@a2683bfb233a:/home/user/config-tracking#

steps.message:
Analysis: Perfect! All tasks have been completed successfully. The git repository has been initialized at /home/user/config-tracking, the app.conf file has been created with the correct content, it has been committed with the exact commit message 'Initial configuration setup', and the setup.log file has been created with exactly 4 lines containing: 1) the full path to the repository, 2) the configuration file name, 3) the full 40-character commit hash, and 4) the commit message.
Plan: Verify the final state by checking the git status and listing the directory contents to confirm everything is in place.

steps.message:
Analysis: All required tasks have been completed successfully: 1) Git repository initialized at /home/user/config-tracking, 2) app.conf file created with the exact specified content, 3) app.conf added to git and committed with the exact commit message 'Initial configuration setup', 4) setup.log file created with exactly 4 lines containing the repository path, configuration file name, full commit hash, and commit message. The setup.log file being untracked is expected as the task only required tracking app.conf.
Plan: The task is complete. All requirements have been met.
